Financial Data

Financial Transparency and Accountability

Kadir Has University maintains a strong commitment to financial transparency and accountability in all its research and development activities. In line with national higher education regulations, the University’s annual R&D financial data are systematically compiled and submitted to the Council of Higher Education (Yükseköğretim Kurulu Başkanlığı) each year. The figures presented below demonstrate our institution’s commitment to transparency, accurate reporting, and compliance with auditing requirements. These data illustrate our transparent use of resources and our accountability in managing both internal and external R&D funds.

Please note that TL stands for Turkish Lira, the official currency of the Republic of Türkiye.

YÖK (HIGHER EDUCATION COUNCIL OF TÜRKİYE) 2024 SUBMITTED FINANCIAL DATA

TABLE 17-B

A. In-House R&D Expenditure

Documents to be Prepared for Audit

2023–2024 (TL)

2024–2025 (TL)

1

Cash Scholarships for Master’s with Thesis & PhD Students (excluding tuition/fees waived by the University; only cash deposited to the student’s account)

The annual total will be calculated over the monthly amounts deposited into the accounts of students pursuing a Master’s with Thesis or a PhD. It covers scholarships deposited in cash to the student’s account, excluding tuition/fees waived by the University.

Data will be prepared on an individual (per-person) basis in an Excel file. Bank receipts will be kept ready, matched with the sequence numbers provided in Excel.

5,154,596.25

5,887,508.49

2

Funds used to support research projects through BAP and similar programs

Enter the amounts transferred to projects supported under programs that are expected—upon completion—to contribute to science in their fields, and to the country’s technological, economic, social and cultural development; that may be carried out with the participation of internal/external, national/international institutions; and that support training of researchers and the establishment/improvement of research infrastructure.

“Data will be prepared on a project basis in an Excel file. The Excel will indicate both the total project budget and the amount transferred within the year. In the same Excel, include an ‘R&D-qualification project summary’ for each project. Project contracts signed between the Rector or Vice Rectors and academic staff will be prepared. The amount transferred within the year under each project will be verified.”

1,540,508.00

438,296.49

3

Performance-based individual research projects and similar funds

Enter the amount of funds provided for research based on academic staff performance.

“Data will be prepared on an individual (per-person) basis in an Excel file. Bank receipts will be kept ready, matched with the sequence numbers provided in Excel.”

4,472,816.51

4,918,651.80

4

Library expenses

The amount spent with the institution’s internal funds for scholarly publications (excluding textbooks, lecture notes, and “textbooks”).

“Data will be prepared on a purchase basis in an Excel file. Bank receipts will be kept ready, matched with the sequence numbers provided in Excel.”

684,172.32

11,899,587.37

5

Research database expenses (as designated by YÖK)

Covers the costs of license agreements concluded with research-database providers designated by YÖK.

“Data will be prepared on a contract basis in an Excel file. Bank receipts will be kept ready, matched with the sequence numbers provided in Excel.”

8,858,532.05

1,312,236.69

6

Researcher mobility supports (including domestic/international conference supports)

Enter the amounts paid—using university internal funds within the relevant year—to Master’s with Thesis/PhD students and academic staff for mobility (assignments for research, courses, training, conferences, symposia, domestic/international).

“Data will be prepared on an individual (per-person) basis in an Excel file. Bank receipts will be kept ready, matched with the sequence numbers provided in Excel.”

251,189.23

1,036,264.97

7

Annual membership fees to international scientific organizations

Annual fees paid for memberships to international scientific organizations.

“Data will be prepared on a contract basis in an Excel file. Bank receipts will be kept ready, matched with the sequence numbers provided in Excel.”

856,045.90

B. Domestic Externally-Funded R&D Expenditure

Total: 17,980,585.00 TL (2023–2024) | 30,342,242.16 TL (2024–2025)

1

Supports of Central Government Organizations (TÜBİTAK—ARDEB/TEYDEB/BİDEB, KOSGEB—R&D, Innovation & Industrial Application, TÜSEB, Development Agencies, MoIT Technology-Focused Industry Move, Defense Industry Presidency, TAGEM, relevant Ministries)

Enter the amounts transferred within the year to projects supported by National R&D and Innovation Support Programs that started, continued, or were completed in the relevant year (including public-supported industry projects in R&D/innovation and consultancy services provided to them; excluding investment projects and BAP projects).

An Excel table will be prepared showing the amount of funds transferred in the year by the relevant public institutions/organizations providing R&D and innovation funds to foundation universities, and indicating under which project the funds were transferred (project name, start–end dates, project budget).

Supports from For-Profit and Not-for-Profit Companies

Enter the amount of funds received from university–industry collaboration R&D and innovation projects funded solely by industrial organizations that started, continued, or were completed in the relevant year.

“Data will be prepared on a project basis in an Excel file. For each project listed in Excel, include an ‘R&D-qualification project summary’ in the same file. As evidence, universities will keep ready the project contracts/assignment letters and documents/invoices showing the transferred amounts, matched with the sequence numbers given in Excel.”

D. Total Externally-Funded R&D Expenditures

168,930,597.23 TL (2023–2024) | 201,611,167.62 TL (2024–2025)

Note:

Apart from these items, additional expenditures by the foundation higher education institution, such as laboratory infrastructure, machinery-equipment purchases, consumables, and personnel costs, may generally be considered within the scope of R&D; however, since a low value like 1% of student income is taken as a reference for tangible and clear R&D outputs, only the items above will be taken into account in calculating the total R&D expenditure.
